## Task: Wire FileServiceOptions + IFileService into DI and fix middleware order
## Step: review-tests

Edit Program.cs only. After `builder.Services.AddControllers();` add (use the right usings or fully-qualified names): `builder.Services.Configure<TestProject.FileServiceOptions>(builder.Configuration.GetSection("FileService"));` and `builder.Services.AddSingleton<TestProject.Services.IFileService, TestProject.Services.FileService>();`. The request pipeline must be EXACTLY in this order: `app.UseHttpsRedirection();` then `app.UseDefaultFiles();` then `app.UseStaticFiles();` then `app.MapControllers();` then `app.MapFallbackToFile("index.html");` then `app.Run();`. (DefaultFiles must come before StaticFiles; the fallback must come after MapControllers.) Keep the rest of the template (CreateBuilder, AddControllers, Build) unchanged. Verify: `dotnet build TestProject.csproj` succeeds; then `dotnet run --project TestProject.csproj` and in another shell confirm `curl -sk https://localhost:7146/` returns HTML (default files) and an unknown path like `https://localhost:7146/some/deep/link` falls back to index.html.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The test project has a critical compilation failure that prevents ALL tests from running. The main project's `Controllers/` directory contains only `TestController.cs` (class `TestController`), but two test files — `FilesControllerTests.cs` and `FilesControllerContractTests.cs` — reference `TestProject.Controllers.FilesController`, which does not exist. This produces a CS0246 error on build (`The type or namespace name 'FilesController' could not be found`), blocking execution of even the tests relevant to this step. The tests that are directly relevant to this task — `ProgramWiringTests.cs` (DI wiring and pipeline ordering) and `FileServiceOptionsTests.cs` (options class) — are well-written: each test is focused on a single behavior, edge cases are covered (exact-count checks, ordering constraints, placement relative to Build), regex-based source parsing is appropriate for verifying Program.cs structure, and test descriptions clearly state expected behavior. No quality issues were found in those test files themselves. The blocking issue is that the two FilesController test files must either be removed, excluded from the build, or the corresponding `FilesController` must exist in the main project before the test suite can compile.

- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.

## Task: Wire FileServiceOptions + IFileService into DI and fix middleware order
## Step: review-tests

Edit Program.cs only. After `builder.Services.AddControllers();` add (use the right usings or fully-qualified names): `builder.Services.Configure<TestProject.FileServiceOptions>(builder.Configuration.GetSection("FileService"));` and `builder.Services.AddSingleton<TestProject.Services.IFileService, TestProject.Services.FileService>();`. The request pipeline must be EXACTLY in this order: `app.UseHttpsRedirection();` then `app.UseDefaultFiles();` then `app.UseStaticFiles();` then `app.MapControllers();` then `app.MapFallbackToFile("index.html");` then `app.Run();`. (DefaultFiles must come before StaticFiles; the fallback must come after MapControllers.) Keep the rest of the template (CreateBuilder, AddControllers, Build) unchanged. Verify: `dotnet build TestProject.csproj` succeeds; then `dotnet run --project TestProject.csproj` and in another shell confirm `curl -sk https://localhost:7146/` returns HTML (default files) and an unknown path like `https://localhost:7146/some/deep/link` falls back to index.html.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The test project has a critical compilation failure that prevents ALL tests from running. The main project's `Controllers/` directory contains only `TestController.cs` (class `TestController`), but two test files — `FilesControllerTests.cs` and `FilesControllerContractTests.cs` — reference `TestProject.Controllers.FilesController`, which does not exist. This produces a CS0246 error on build (`The type or namespace name 'FilesController' could not be found`), blocking execution of even the tests relevant to this step. The tests that are directly relevant to this task — `ProgramWiringTests.cs` (DI wiring and pipeline ordering) and `FileServiceOptionsTests.cs` (options class) — are well-written: each test is focused on a single behavior, edge cases are covered (exact-count checks, ordering constraints, placement relative to Build), regex-based source parsing is appropriate for verifying Program.cs structure, and test descriptions clearly state expected behavior. No quality issues were found in those test files themselves. The blocking issue is that the two FilesController test files must either be removed, excluded from the build, or the corresponding `FilesController` must exist in the main project before the test suite can compile.
Attempt 2: The test suite remains unbuildable due to the same critical issue identified previously: FilesControllerTests.cs and FilesControllerContractTests.cs reference a FilesController class that does not exist in the main project, causing CS0246 build errors. The tests directly relevant to the current task (ProgramWiringTests.cs and FileServiceOptionsTests.cs) are well-constructed and appropriate, but they cannot be executed because the test project fails to compile. Until the FilesController dependency is resolved (by either creating the class, removing the test files, or excluding them from the build), no tests can run. The implementation of the required Program.cs changes has not been performed, so the task itself is incomplete.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.
